notes: Exact headline sentence written (README blockquote): 'Headline claim (pre-registered RQ3) - HELD: a class-calibrated effort policy uses 64.7% fewer output tokens (bootstrap 95% CI [60.9, 67.7]) than the status-quo inheritance policy (every subagent at xhigh), at equal aggregate pass rate (1.000 vs 1.000). It also beat the model-default uniform-high by 20.5% fewer tokens [12.3, 27.8], and out-scored the uniform-low heuristic by +8.3 pp aggregate pass (1.000 vs 0.917) - so it is un-dominated on the pre-registered Pareto test. This was a real test that could have failed; a null or negative result would have shipped as-is.' Honest caveats travel with every numbers surface (README results section, both miner-low/miner-xhigh descriptions, SKILL.md fallback JSON T4 caveat + closing line, CHANGELOG notes): n=3/cell => per-class confidence low; T4 low rests on pre-registered-misclassification-flagged (too-easy-for-Opus-4.8) tasks, harder T4 queued; T3 is the class with the real gradient (low 6/9, high sweet spot at 157 med tok vs max's 648); single model / prompt-only / one machine. SKILL.md fallback table now mirrors v1 EXACTLY (T1 low, T2 low, T3 high, T4 low + inline caveat that a cautious user prefers xhigh for genuinely hard work until the suite is extended), relabeled 'fitted pilot v1 snapshot (2026-07-06)' - it is no longer an unproven pre-pilot ladder but the no-file fallback still. All five miner descriptions updated: miner-low names T1/T2 + T4-caveat; miner-high names T3; miner-medium/xhigh/max state that no class currently calibrates to them and when a caller should still pick them (medium = one-step hedge above low; xhigh/max = tasks harder than the pilot suite; max also carries the overthinking-tail warning).
